name: Scythe-Fiend
physical: Dog-sized, four legs, two scything forelimbs tipped with acid-coated chitin blades, and a head that is mostly mouth — a circular maw of interlocking chitinous teeth evolved for rapid biomass consumption. The most numerous ground organism: cheap, fast, expendable. Pour across the battlefield in waves with a high-pitched chittering that increases in frequency as the swarm closes distance.
narrative: A single burst kills one. Thirty charging across open ground overwhelm any unprepared defensive position. Acid-coated forelimbs deal moderate damage plus a 3-second acid DOT that degrades armor. The swarm's basic fuel — quantity over quality, the Vorax foundational doctrine.

name: Acid-Belcher
physical: Bloated toad-like organism the size of a compact car. Four stumpy legs barely sufficient for its mass. Massively distended throat contains a biological acid reservoir. Projects a concentrated acid stream — viscous yellow-green liquid arcing through air with a 5m splash radius on impact. Acid pools persist 10 seconds, dealing 3% HP/sec to all non-Vorax units.
narrative: Critical area-denial. Acid pools block chokepoints, deny cover positions, force repositioning — often directly into advancing Scythe-Fiends. Slow, poorly armored, relies entirely on swarm screening for survival.

name: Burrow-Terror
physical: 3m serpentine eyeless worm with sensory tendrils and hardened digging spines. Lives underground detecting vibrations through substrate. Erupts beneath targets in a shower of soil, wraps and constricts for 3 seconds while boring spines penetrate even reinforced armor. Completely invisible until the moment of attack.
narrative: Every step forward might trigger an ambush. Can be deployed defensively — burrowed beneath a territory, waiting motionless for enemies to advance overhead. First warning: tremor. Second: ground breaking. No third warning.

name: Synapse-Brain
voraxNote: PRIORITY TARGET. Killing the Synapse-Brain triggers FRENZY state in all nearby Vorax: mindless aggression, attacks include allies, erratic movement, 1% HP/sec decay. Also triggers Eternal Shards Psychic Echo feedback (8% HP to nearby Shards units). Kills the Vorax as a coordinated threat.
physical: Floating bio-luminescent organism — a massive exposed brain in a membranous sac of cerebrospinal fluid. Gas bladders provide lift. Trailing neural tendrils connect telepathically to all organisms within 100m. NO combat capability whatsoever. Dies to a single concentrated burst. Always positioned behind the swarm line, surrounded by a Scythe-Fiend bodyguard ring.
narrative: THE priority target. While alive: tactical coherence — flanking, focus fire, terrain use, coordinated retreat. When killed: all organisms enter FRENZY — mindless aggression, attack nearest target including allies, erratic movement, 1% HP/sec decay. Death is announced by a bioluminescent pulse — the closest the Vorax get to expressing pain.

name: Bio-Titan
voraxNote: DEATH IS A HAZARD EVENT. On death: body ruptures, releasing thousands of liters of pressurized bio-acid in a 20m radius. Units must be pulled AWAY from a dying Titan rather than clustering for the kill.
physical: Six-legged three-story biological war engine. Chitin exoskeleton over pulsating tissue. Head: massive bio-plasma cannon rivaling conventional artillery. Legs end in crushing talons. Body: spine-launchers on flanks, spore-sacs dorsally, acid glands under jaw. Each plasma shot creates a persistent bio-acid pool providing 15-second area denial.
narrative: Deployed against fortifications the swarm cannot overwhelm through mass alone. As dangerous dead as alive — the death rupture radius must be factored into every engagement decision. Crowding for the kill costs more than the kill is worth.

name: Gargoyle-Bat
physical: Bat-sized flying organisms in flocks of 20–30 functioning as a single coordinated unit. Individually insignificant. Collectively: descend on enemy aircraft like living shrapnel, clogging engine intakes, cracking cockpit glass, coating sensor arrays in viscous mucus.
narrative: Air superiority through suffocation. Cheap, expendable, and overwhelming in numbers. Other factions' fighter pilots are trained to avoid Gargoyle-Bat clouds rather than engage them — the swarm mechanics make individual dogfighting suicidal.

name: Venom-Spitter
physical: Horse-sized flying organism with broad chitin wings. Ventral acid gland sprays corrosive venom in strafing runs. Each pass leaves a 30m acid trail persisting 5 seconds. Multiple Spitters operating in sequence create overlapping denial zones across entire approach corridors.
narrative: Strafing acid runs deny entire ground sectors. Infantry caught in the open during a Venom-Spitter sortie learn quickly why the Vorax win wars of attrition.

name: Harpy-Spore
physical: Bloated gas-bladder organism maintaining high altitude, releasing Spore Pods — chitin-parachute containers that embed in the ground on contact. Proximity mines: rupture within 3m of any non-Vorax unit releasing viral spores that inflict −10% accuracy and −10% speed for 15 seconds. Deploys 8–12 pods per sortie.
narrative: Seeds approach routes with biological minefields. Entire advance corridors become infection zones. The Vorax don't need to be present to stop an enemy advance.

name: Spore-Cloud
physical: 30m living dirigible — hydrogen gas bladders above, organism cargo below. Deploys cargo through ventral sphincters — organisms scattered across a 20m radius landing zone. EXTREMELY vulnerable to anti-air. A single incendiary round converts it into a hydrogen fireball raining burning Vorax.
narrative: Shooting down a loaded Spore-Cloud is one of the most satisfying and horrifying moments in the game. The defenders who survive the flaming Vorax rain rarely forget it.

name: Leviathan-Transport
voraxNote: CAMPAIGN PRIORITY TARGET. Broadcasts enhanced coordination signal equivalent to 2 Synapse-Brains. Destroying it cripples the entire Vorax ground campaign on that planet.
physical: Whale-sized bio-ship. Carries entire planetary invasion forces. Broadcasts enhanced hive coordination signal equivalent to two Synapse-Brains — the most powerful coordination aura in the Vorax ground inventory. Heavily armored chitin resisting all but sustained heavy-weapons fire. Escorted by Gargoyle-Bat swarms at all times.
narrative: Campaign-level priority target. Coordination signal and transport capacity make it the backbone of any Vorax planetary invasion. Destroying it cripples the invasion's tactical coherence — the swarm loses its distributed intelligence and devolves into frenzy.

name: Sporeshot
physical: Living torpedo organism launched from Hive-Ships. Accelerates under biological propulsion, IMPACTS target hull, bores through plating with chitinous drill-proboscis, then releases corrosive organisms that consume the ship from the inside. A single Sporeshot is an annoyance requiring an internal response team. A salvo of 20 is a crisis — crew must fight a biological infestation in the middle of a fleet engagement.
narrative: The space equivalent of being eaten alive from the inside. The most psychologically disturbing attack in the game for the crew experiencing it.

name: Leech-Corvette
physical: Medium bio-ship with grappling tendrils designed for attachment to enemy hulls. While attached: drains −30% shields, −20% weapon cycling speed, −15% engine thrust. All drained energy converts to Biomass fed back to the nearest Hive-Ship. Minimal direct combat capability but exceptionally durable while attached. Requires close-range concentrated fire to remove.
narrative: Attached Leeches transform capital ships into sluggish, weakened prey for Parasite-Krakens. The combination is the Vorax's primary capital-killing doctrine: drain first, grapple second.

name: Parasite-Kraken
physical: Deep-sea predator silhouette — elongated armored body, massive head, trailing tentacles tipped with armor-piercing chitin spikes. WRAPS tentacles around capital ships, constricting and piercing simultaneously. Each spike bores through armor and releases acid into the ship's internal systems. Destroys a capital ship in 30–40 seconds if not driven off by concentrated point-defense fire. No ranged weapons — must survive approach fire to engage.
narrative: Getting the Kraken into grappling range requires a full escort operation: Leeches draining weapons and shields, Sporeshots distracting point defense. Once grappled, the clock is ticking and every second matters.

name: Bile-Sprayer
physical: Broad flat bio-ship with a ventral organ projecting a wide cone of bio-acid into vacuum. Acid cloud persists 10 seconds — damages any ship passing through the zone. Blocks warp entry lanes, denies approach vectors, and is devastating against fighters whose thin hulls dissolve on contact. Denies entire quadrants to fighter operations.
narrative: The space equivalent of Creep — area denial that shapes the entire battlefield geometry before conventional weapons fire. An enemy fleet that ignores Bile-Sprayers finds itself with no safe approach.

name: Hive-Ship
voraxNote: PRIMARY OBJECTIVE. Destroying the Hive-Ship kills the entire Vorax Tendril. Without it: cannot coordinate, spawn, or process Biomass. Destruction triggers spectacular chain-reaction bioluminescent detonation visible from planetary surfaces.
physical: Small-moon-sized. A living city of spawning chambers, Biomass processing organs, and Hive Mind coordination arrays. DESTROYING IT KILLS THE TENDRIL. Exterior: spine-launchers (torpedo-sized), acid glands, Sporeshot tubes (standard salvo: 20). Thickest chitin in the Vorax inventory — layered, continuously regenerating, hybrid organic-metallic composition.
narrative: The most important military objective in any Vorax engagement. Surrounded by the entire escort fleet, screened by Bile-Sprayer clouds, defended by Gargoyle swarms and Krakens. Reaching it means fighting through multiple layers of evolved biological defense. When destroyed: chain-reaction bioluminescent detonation visible from planetary surfaces. Then silence. The Tendril is dead. Until the next one spawns.
